title: Dashboard-driven operations model
description: Implement persistent agents, alert/status history, maintenance suppression, pending enrollment workflow, authentication, RBAC groups, dashboard status/alert UI behavior, webhook notifications, and Windows EXE installers.
acceptance_criteria: Server restarts keep enrolled agents and all mutable runtime state by using a stable persisted SQLite database path.
Approved agents are separated from pending enrollments in API and dashboard views.
Status history records every indicator transition with timestamps.
Alerts are generated only on worsening transitions, are suppressed and cleared by maintenance, are soft-deleted, and are acknowledged by users.
Dashboard overview shows approved agents only, with Healthy | Warning | Degraded | Critical | Maintenance | Offline counters.
Indicator dots show grey for no data, blue for maintenance, and the derived Status column prioritizes red > amber > yellow > grey > green.
Agents page manages approved agents only and can enter/exit maintenance with optional reason and expiry.
Pending Enrollments page lets admins approve/reject and assign groups on approval.
Login uses secure local SQLite-backed sessions with default admin credentials documented as thewatcher/look_at_me.
Users, groups, roles, user-group membership, and agent-group membership enforce visibility and permissions.
Windows EXE installers install server and agent as services under C:\Program Files\TheWatcher\.
Docs, changelog, version, and BDD tests are updated.

tasks.title: Persistence foundation
tasks.description: Make server persistence stable across restarts, extend store records for maintenance metadata and group membership, and add backend schema for users, groups, alerts, status history, webhook config, and sessions.
acceptance_criteria: Relative server db_path resolves beside the active server config file.
Agent mutable state persists: connected/offline, maintenance, maintenance reason/expiry, runtime settings, groups, active alerts.
Store schema can represent alerts, status history, users, groups, roles, sessions, and webhook URL/config.
Existing SQLite databases migrate in place without dropping agents.

unit: GIVEN a server config with relative db_path WHEN it is loaded or created THEN db_path resolves under the config directory.
GIVEN a store with agent maintenance metadata and groups WHEN it is reopened THEN all fields are retained.
GIVEN alert and status history rows WHEN queried THEN soft-deleted alerts are hidden from active results.

tasks.implementation_notes: Touch server/config.cpp,hpp, server/store.hpp, server/store_sqlite.cpp,hpp, server_tests/store_test.cpp, server_tests/config tests if present. Avoid destructive migrations. Use ALTER TABLE for agent columns and CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS for new entities.

subtasks.title: Stable SQLite path
subtasks.description: Resolve relative ServerConfig::db_path against the active config directory so restarts from different working directories reuse the same SQLite database.
acceptance_criteria: Relative db_path in a loaded config becomes config_directory/db_path.
First-run default config writes an absolute db_path under the config directory.
Absolute db_path values are preserved.

unit: GIVEN a temp config directory WHEN load_or_create creates the config THEN db_path is temp_dir/thewatcher.db.
GIVEN an existing config with relative db_path WHEN loaded THEN db_path is resolved against that config directory.

subtasks.implementation_notes: Update ServerConfig::load_or_create after JSON decode and before save. Use std::filesystem::absolute/path logic without assuming Windows-only paths.

subtasks.title: Operations schema
subtasks.description: Add persisted schema and store methods for groups, user auth records, sessions, status history, alerts, and webhook config.
acceptance_criteria: Tables exist for users, groups, user_groups, agent_groups, sessions, status_history, alerts, and server_settings.
Default admin user and Admins group are bootstrapped idempotently.
Alerts include agent, indicator, old_status, new_status, message, created_at, acknowledged_by, acknowledged_at, deleted_at.
Status history includes agent, indicator, old_status, new_status, created_at, and message.

unit: GIVEN an empty store WHEN schema initializes THEN default admin and Admins group exist.
GIVEN an alert is soft deleted WHEN active alerts are listed THEN it is omitted but historical query can include it.

subtasks.implementation_notes: Password storage must use a password hash, not plaintext. Prefer libsodium password hashing because libsodium is already a dependency.

tasks.title: Status engine and alerting
tasks.description: Evaluate latest metrics against configurable thresholds based on the average of the last five minutes, record all transitions, and generate alerts only when an indicator worsens.
acceptance_criteria: Thresholds are configurable per indicator as percentages of the five-minute average.
Green means Healthy, Yellow means Warning, Amber means Degraded, Red means Critical/Failure, Grey means No Data, Blue means Maintenance.
Only worsening transitions create alerts.
Recoveries and lateral changes are recorded in status history but do not create alerts.
Maintenance suppresses and clears active alerts for the agent.
Webhook notifications are sent for generated alerts when configured.

tasks.title: Enrollment and agent APIs
tasks.description: Split pending enrollment from approved agent management and require group assignment on approval.
acceptance_criteria: Overview and agents APIs expose approved agents only.
Pending enrollment API exposes pending agents to admins only.
Approve endpoint accepts group ids and stores agent-group membership.
Rejected/deleted pending agents can re-enroll.

tasks.title: Authentication and RBAC
tasks.description: Add local SQLite-backed login, secure sessions, default admin credentials, users, groups, and role permissions.
acceptance_criteria: Default admin login is username thewatcher and password look_at_me.
Passwords are hashed with libsodium.
Sessions use secure HTTP-only cookies with CSRF-conscious mutation handling.
Roles admin/operator/viewer gate actions.
Users see union access across their groups; built-in admin sees all groups.

tasks.title: Dashboard workflow
tasks.description: Implement overview, approved agents, pending enrollments, alerts, login, user/group management, status/alert columns, counters, and maintenance interactions.
acceptance_criteria: Top counters are Healthy | Warning | Degraded | Critical | Maintenance | Offline.
Approval column is removed.
Overview and Agents pages exclude pending enrollments.
Pending Enrollments page displays only unapproved agents awaiting admin action.
No-data indicators are grey and derived host Status is yellow when any required indicator has no data.
Alerts dot is green unless the host has unacknowledged alerts, then red.
Thin unacknowledged alert banner appears between counters and server list.
Alerts page supports acknowledge and soft delete.
Maintenance mode turns indicators blue and suppresses/clears alerts.

tasks.title: Windows EXE installers
tasks.description: Build Windows EXE installers for server and agent that install binaries under C:\Program Files\TheWatcher\ and register services automatically.
acceptance_criteria: Server installer installs TheWatcherServer as a Windows service.
Agent installer installs TheWatcherAgent as a Windows service.
Config and log paths remain writable and documented.
Installer docs describe install, upgrade, uninstall, and default admin login.

edges.label: Stable DB location before broader schema.
Dashboard status depends on evaluated backend state.
Dashboard pages depend on split APIs.
Installer docs include default admin/auth behavior.
